pattern: Because every ride is really just a weighted interval on the line of points, and a picked-up ride can never overlap another, the best total earnings up to each point only depends on the best total at the point where that ride began -- a point-indexed running best turns this into ordinary interval scheduling.
hints: Group the rides by their drop-off point, since a ride can only ever affect the running best exactly at the point it ends.
Sweep the points from 1 to n, carrying forward a running best-earnings-so-far; at each point, the best without taking any ride ending there is simply the running best from the previous point.

For every ride ending at the current point, taking it is worth the running best at its starting point (from a previous, already-computed point) plus its own profit; keep whichever option -- skip, or one of the rides ending here -- is largest.

edge_inputs.description: a single ride spanning almost the whole route
several rides chained end to end with none overlapping
the smallest possible route with exactly one ride
a single ride with the maximum possible tip
one long ride looks tempting but two shorter chained rides earn strictly more
a ride's drop-off exactly matches another's pick-up, which must be allowed back to back
identical fully-overlapping rides where only one can ever be taken

stress.description: random valid rides scattered across a large route
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 100000))
    num_rides = max(1, min(30000, n * 2))
    rides = []
    for _ in range(num_rides):
        s = rng.randint(1, n - 1)
        e = rng.randint(s + 1, n)
        t = rng.randint(1, 100000)
        rides.append([s, e, t])
    return [n, rides]
